context: Sunday, June 7 — weekend pre-market, thin liquidity, basket relief-bouncing off Friday's capitulation. The week was a synchronized risk-off flush: Friday's hot NFP (+172K vs 85K forecast) repriced Fed cuts further out and, stacked on an 11-session record ETF outflow streak (~$3.4B, largest since the 2024 launch) and MicroStrategy's first-ever BTC sale, drove BTC through the $60K line to an intraday low ~$60,288 — ~$3B of leverage liquidated over two days and perp OI flushed -8.5% to ~$111.4B. Fear & Greed sits at 12 (Extreme Fear), barely off the June-3 reading of 11. Live tape today: BTC $62,759 (+2.18% 24h, -14.93% 7d), XRP $1.15 (+4.24%, -14.03% 7d), SUI $0.759 (+6.64%, -16.48% 7d — weakest on the week from its triple-outage damage), HBAR $0.0822 (+3.68%, -14.02% 7d). The whole basket is green on 24h but the move is a thin-Sunday mean-reversion off oversold, not a confirmed reversal. $60,000 BTC is THE line — hold it and the path is back toward $64K-$66K supply; a daily close below reopens $57K/$55K. CLARITY remains the lone structural tailwind: one Senate floor vote (expected mid-June) from making XRP a statutory digital commodity, White House targeting a Jul 4 signing, with XRP spot ETFs (~$1.40B cumulative inflows) the relative-strength tell vs BTC's outflows. Trade plan: ZERO momentum chasing into the Sunday wick; wait for Monday US-session confirmation of the $60K reclaim. SUI stays SHORT (fade $0.80-0.85 supply). Next macro binary: FOMC Jun 16-17 (HOLD priced firmer post-NFP); CPI mid-June is the warm-up.

tickers.thesis: BTC broke the $60K line Friday (intraday low ~$60,288) on a hot NFP + record ETF outflows, then reclaimed it — now $62,759 (+2.18% 24h, but -14.93% on the week). Friday's flush wiped ~$3B of leverage over two days and dropped perp OI -8.5% to ~$111.4B, so the bounce is standing on a much cleaner base. This is a weekend relief move off Extreme-Fear (F&G 12), not a confirmed reversal: thin Sunday liquidity exaggerates the candle. $60,000 is now THE line — hold it and the path is back toward the $64K-$66K supply shelf; daily close below $60K reopens $57K then $55K. Drivers still net-bearish: 11-session ETF outflow streak (~$3.4B, largest since launch), MicroStrategy's first-ever sale, hot NFP pushing Fed cuts out. Trade plan: don't chase the Sunday wick — wait for Monday US-session confirmation of the $60K reclaim; longs only on a hold, size small with stop below $59.5K.

tickers.thesis: XRP got dragged down with the macro tape (-14% on the week, low $1.073) but is bouncing hardest of the majors today (+4.24%) on its own structural tailwind: the CLARITY Act is one Senate floor vote from making XRP a statutory digital commodity, expected mid-June, with the White House publicly targeting a Jul 4 signing. The relative-strength tell remains the seven US spot XRP ETFs holding ~$1.40B cumulative inflows — money flowing IN even as BTC ETFs bleed out. That divergence is why XRP stays HIGH priority and NEUTRAL (not short) despite the drawdown. Tactically the weekend bounce is into resistance: $1.20 (old support, now overhead) caps the near-term; reclaiming it opens $1.27. Downside the $1.07 Friday low is the line. Don't pre-position size into a thin Sunday — let the Senate-floor headline cycle and Monday flows set direction; the CLARITY vote is the binary that re-rates this name.

tickers.thesis: SUI is the weakest name in the basket (-16.5% on the week, low $0.688) and the weakness is structural, not just macro beta: the network halted three times in under 48 hours (May 28-Jun 1, >15h total downtime) from a v1.72 upgrade bug, and the Jun 1 unlock added 23.78M SUI of fresh supply into a fragile tape. Today's +6.64% is the biggest 24h bounce in the basket — but it's a dead-cat off oversold, not a repair of the reliability damage. A chain that stalls three times on one upgrade carries a lasting confidence discount that bleeds the multiple. Bias stays SHORT: fade strength into the $0.80-$0.85 supply (where the breakdown began), stop above $0.90. The $0.688 low is the pivot — lose it and $0.65/$0.60 open up. Only a sustained reclaim of $0.90 with no further outages flips the structural read.

tickers.thesis: HBAR was the lone green name a week ago but got pulled into the macro flush like everything else (-14% on the week, low $0.0778); it's now bouncing +3.68% with the basket. The bull case is slow-burn institutional, not price momentum: the Canary spot HBAR ETF (Nasdaq: HBR) holds ~549M tokens / ~$93M AUM, HBAR just listed a direct JPY pair on OKCoin Japan (first access to a tightly-regulated market), and the Governing Council keeps adding enterprise names (Google, IBM, FedEx, McLaren/F1). None of that is a today-catalyst, so bias stays NEUTRAL: this is a range trade, not a breakout, until price clears $0.11. Near-term the $0.0778 Friday low is support and the $0.090-$0.10 band is the cap. Accumulation-zone buyers can scale the $0.078-$0.082 demand with a stop below $0.077; the thesis only changes character on a decisive $0.11 break.

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 12 (Extreme Fear), Δ +0 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 12 (Extreme Fear) at 09:06Z — +0 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Cross-exchange divergence on XRP: 0.59%
insights.body: Multi-source price triangulation (CoinGecko + Binance + Bybit) shows XRP divergence of 0.59% at 09:06Z. Divergence >0.5% across major venues is a thin-liquidity / arb-window signal. At 0.59% it's still in normal range but worth monitoring — widening = exit signal for sized trades on XRP.

insights.title: Capitulation Low Printed Friday on the $60K Break — Bounce, Not Yet Reversal
insights.body: Friday's hot NFP (+172K vs 85K) was the trigger that finally cracked the $60K line — BTC tagged ~$60,288 intraday, ~$3B of leverage was liquidated over two days, and perp OI flushed -8.5% to ~$111.4B. F&G sits at 12 (Extreme Fear). That is a textbook capitulation profile, and the whole basket is now bouncing off it (everything green on 24h). BUT this is a thin-Sunday relief move off oversold — confirmation requires a Monday US-session hold of the $60K reclaim with volume. Trade plan: NO new longs until that confirmation; if it holds, $60,300-$61,000 is the OTE reload toward $64K-$66K. If BTC loses $60K on a daily close, the leg extends to $57K then $55K. Don't front-run the weekend candle.

insights.title: XRP Is the Relative-Strength Tell — ETF Inflows Diverging From BTC Outflows
insights.body: XRP fell with the macro tape (-14% on the week, low $1.073) but is bouncing hardest of the majors today (+4.24%) and carries the only clean structural bid in the basket: the seven US spot XRP ETFs hold ~$1.40B cumulative inflows — money flowing IN while BTC ETFs bled a record ~$3.4B out. That divergence keeps XRP NEUTRAL (not short) and HIGH priority. The CLARITY Act is one Senate floor vote from making XRP a statutory digital commodity (expected mid-June; White House targets a Jul 4 signing) — that vote is the binary that re-rates the name. Tactically the bounce is into resistance: $1.20 (old support flipped) caps near-term, reclaim opens $1.27; $1.073 is the downside line.

insights.title: SUI's Weakness Is Structural, Not Just Macro Beta — Stay Short Into Strength
insights.body: SUI is the worst name in the basket (-16.5% on the week) for a reason beyond risk-off: the network halted three times in under 48 hours (May 28-Jun 1, >15h downtime) from a v1.72 upgrade bug, and the Jun 1 unlock added 23.78M SUI of fresh supply. Today's +6.64% is the biggest 24h bounce in the basket — a dead-cat off oversold, not a repair of the reliability damage. A chain that stalls three times on one upgrade carries a lasting confidence discount. Bias stays SHORT: fade strength into the $0.80-$0.85 supply where the breakdown began, stop above $0.90. The $0.688 Friday low is the pivot — lose it and $0.65/$0.60 open. Only a sustained $0.90 reclaim with no further outages flips the read.

insights.title: HBAR Lost Its Lone-Green Status to the Flush — Range Trade Until $0.11
insights.body: HBAR was the only green name a week ago but got pulled into the macro flush (-14% on the week, low $0.0778); it's bouncing +3.68% with the basket today. The bull case is slow-burn institutional, not momentum: the Canary spot HBAR ETF (Nasdaq: HBR) holds ~549M tokens / ~$93M AUM, HBAR just added a direct JPY pair on OKCoin Japan, and the Governing Council keeps adding enterprise names (Google, IBM, FedEx, McLaren/F1). None of that is a today-catalyst, so bias stays NEUTRAL — this is a range trade between the $0.0778 Friday low and the $0.090-$0.10 cap until price decisively clears $0.11. insights.title: NFP Resolved Hawkish — The Macro Headwind Is Now Confirmed Into FOMC
insights.body: Friday's jobs print was the week's binary and it landed hot: +172K vs an 85K forecast, unemployment steady at 4.3%, AHE +0.3% m/m / +3.4% y/y. A resilient labour market gives the Fed cover to keep rates restrictive, so cut-odds repriced further out — a headwind for crypto even though it's a 'good economy' print. That sets up FOMC Jun 16-17 as the next macro gate, now firmly priced for a HOLD, with CPI mid-June as the warm-up. Until the Fed path softens, every basket bounce is a tactical mean-reversion against a macro-bearish backdrop — size accordingly and respect the $60K BTC line as the regime switch.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 12 (Extreme Fear), Δ +0 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm
XRP cross-exchange divergence 0.59% — thin-liquidity tell, watch for further widening as exit signal

BTC spot ETFs posted a record weekly outflow ~$3.4B — the largest since the January-2024 launch, an 11-session bleed led by IBIT/FBTC; this structural exit (not retail panic) is the single biggest driver of the week's break and the thing that must reverse for a durable bottom

MicroStrategy's first-ever BTC sale (32 BTC, ~$2.5M, to fund preferred-stock distributions) — tiny in size but a psychological regime break after years of accumulation-only; watch whether it's a one-off funding mechanic or the start of a behaviour change among corporate holders

Hot NFP (172K vs 85K) repriced Fed-cut odds further out — a rare case where a STRONG economy print is bearish for crypto because it removes the rate-cut tailwind; the macro headwind is now confirmed into FOMC Jun 16-17, so bounces are tactical against a restrictive Fed

Perp OI flushed -8.5% to ~$111.4B with ~$3B liquidated over two days — leverage has been reset, which is constructive: the relief bounce is standing on a much cleaner base than the pre-Friday tape, lowering the odds of an immediate cascade continuation

Seven US spot XRP ETFs hold ~$1.40B cumulative inflows and kept attracting money through the drawdown — a clean relative-strength divergence vs BTC ETF outflows; this institutional bid is why XRP bounces hardest (+4.24%) and stays NEUTRAL rather than short

The whole basket is down a near-uniform -14% to -16.5% on the week — that tight clustering is the signature of macro beta (a single risk-off driver), not idiosyncratic stories; it means the bounce will be led by macro (BTC/$60K reclaim), and stock-pickers should fade the weakest (SUI)

SUI's third outage of 2026 (3 halts in <48h from one v1.72 upgrade bug) is a structural reliability signal that standard price feeds miss — a chain that stalls repeatedly on a single upgrade carries a lasting multiple discount; the +6.64% bounce doesn't repair that

Weekend green across the basket off F&G 12 (Extreme Fear) is a classic oversold mean-reversion attempt through thin Sunday liquidity — the tell is Monday's US session: follow-through above the Friday lows confirms the bounce, a fade back through them reopens the leg
